commit.message:
Fix CalDAV 405 by registering PROPFIND and REPORT with chi

chi immediately returns 405 for any HTTP method not in its internal
methodMap. Since PROPFIND and REPORT are non-standard, chi rejected
them before even attempting route matching, so requests to /caldav/
never reached the mount handler. Calling chi.RegisterMethod for both
methods adds them to methodMap and mALL, causing the /caldav/ mount
to correctly dispatch WebDAV requests to dispatchCalDAV.

commit.message:
sbd: fix SBD→MBD register packet never sent on startup

chan_set_write_interest() uses a global epoll_fd set only on the
first chan_epoll() call (in the daemon main loop).  sbd_register()
was called during sbd_init(), before the main loop started, so
epoll_fd was still -1 and EPOLLOUT was never registered; the
register packet sat in the send buffer forever, leaving the host
in "unreach" state.

Fix: add chan_init_epoll(fd) to expose the initialisation step,
and call it in sbd_init_network() right after epoll_create1() so
that subsequent chan_set_write_interest() calls work immediately.

commit.message:
Initial research artifact: lit review, schema, and annotated papers

Three literature reviews (~70 papers total):
- Cognitive science of note-taking during reading (30 papers)
- Data formats for multimodal learning traces (xAPI, WADM, Caliper, MMLA)
- Context-aware OCR and LLM-augmented handwriting recognition (30+ papers)

Learning trace schema (JSON Schema + Supabase SQL):
- Three-layer architecture: behavioral events, sensor streams, derived analytics
- W3C WADM TextQuoteSelector for document anchoring
- Think-aloud coding scheme for annotation motivation classification
- Validated against 3 example trace files

10 key papers downloaded from arXiv with 87-entry BibTeX file.

- Team page hero: changed from split (with image) to banner (text-only)

  load in FloatingToolbar (only loads when edit mode is active)

Add CUDA alloc conf + reduce seq lengths for L4 memory

Fix slide teleporting: update hitbox height immediately on slide start

Root cause: p.h was still PLAYER_H (32) on the frame slide starts because
it's only updated at the top of updatePlayer() based on p.isSliding, which
was false at that point. After p.y drops 14px but h stays 32, the player
clips into the ground platform. Horizontal collision then pushes the player
to the platform edge (opposite to movement direction), causing the teleport.

Fix: set p.h = PLAYER_H_SLIDE immediately when slide triggers, so the
hitbox (18px) fits exactly above the ground with no overlap.

fix: move CARGO_TARGET_DIR to /tmp, delete on adapter exit

target/ inside worktrees is 1.8 GB per job and was never cleaned up,
accumulating indefinitely (9 GB freed manually to illustrate the problem).

- dispatcher: target dir is now /tmp/bop-target-<id>/, outside the workspace
- dispatcher: rm -rf target_dir immediately after adapter exits
- merge_gate AC: CARGO_TARGET_DIR → /tmp/bop-target-ac-<id>/, deleted after loop
- merge_gate JJ: already fixed to rm -rf workspace; now target never lives there

Result: worktrees contain only source code (~87 MB), deleted at merge.
At 100k jobs: before = 180 TB accumulated; after = ~0 (cleaned on exit).

fix: add Firestore security rules for apiTokens collection

The apiTokens collection lacked security rules, causing permission-denied
errors when users tried to generate API tokens. Also adds firestore.indexes.json
with the composite index required for token validation lookups, firebase.json
to wire up deployment, and a clearer permission-denied error message in the UI.

Deploy rules with: firebase deploy --only firestore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent duplicate telegram message processing across connectors

Both telegram_bot and telegram_user_client connectors now use a canonical
idempotency key format (tg:<chat_id>:<message_id>) so the same Telegram
message produces the same dedup key regardless of which connector submits it.

Additionally, the switchboard ingest layer now performs a secondary
content-hash dedup check when the primary key doesn't match, catching
cross-connector duplicates even if key normalization isn't perfect.
